compiled_state.title: Fix _graph.py: treat deleted tombstone status as terminal for ready_to_work
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Context
`_graph.py` determines `ready_to_work` by checking each blocker's status. The current check (`if status != "closed":`) does not treat "deleted" as terminal, so blocked tickets remain not-ready even when their blocker has been deleted via a `.tombstone.json`. T1 adds a failing test for this; this task fixes it.

## What to do
Change line ~100 in `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket_graph/_graph.py`:

Before:
```python
if status != "closed":
    ready_to_work = False
```

After:
```python
if status not in ("closed", "deleted"):
    ready_to_work = False
```

One-line change only.

## Files to modify
- `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket_graph/_graph.py` — line ~100, one-line fix

## Acceptance criteria
- Exactly one line changed in `_graph.py`
- T1's test (`test_ready_to_work_when_blocker_has_deleted_tombstone`) passes after this change
- All existing `_graph.py` tests continue to pass (no regressions)

## Depends on
T1 (6b38-8633) — the RED test must exist before this GREEN fix is written

## Testing mode
UPDATE — fixing existing behavior (ready_to_work not clearing when blocker is deleted)

comments.body: GAP_ANALYSIS AC_AMENDMENT: T2 AC must explicitly verify that the .tombstone.json path (not just event log) surfaces through _get_ticket_status. _status.py line 32 reads .tombstone.json and returns tombstone['status'] — this IS the path T1's test exercises. Add assertion in T2's test validation: after writing .tombstone.json with {status:deleted}, call _get_ticket_status directly and assert it returns 'deleted' before running the full graph assertion. This ensures the tombstone-aware status path is the one being tested.
comments.author: Test
